name: Texas Barbecue Pot Roast
recipeIngredient: 4 1/2 Pound eye of round, bottom round,
1 1 1/2 Cups water for beef OR 2 cups water for pork
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the barbecue sauce by mixing together catsup, apricot preserves, dark brown sugar, white vinegar, teriyaki or soy sauce, crushed dry red pepper, dry mustard, and black pepper.
2. Place the roast and barbecue sauce in a large plastic bag or glass dish. Refrigerate overnight.
3. In a 6-quart Presto pressure cooker, place water and set cooking rack on top of it. Add half of the sliced onions to the rack.
4. Remove the roast from the sauce and reserve the sauce. Place the roast on the onions in the pressure cooker, covering with the remaining onions.
5. Securely close the pressure cooker cover. Attach the pressure regulator to the vent pipe.
6. Cook at 15 pounds pressure until the following internal temperatures are reached:
- Rare: 8-10 minutes per pound
- Medium: 10-12 minutes per pound
- Well-done: at least 12-15 minutes per pound for meat
- Pork should be cooked for 15 minutes per pound to ensure it is well done.
7. Allow the pressure to release naturally.
8. While the roast is cooling, place the reserved barbecue sauce in a saucepan and simmer over low heat until reduced by about half, stirring occasionally.
9. Remove the roast and keep warm. Discard any cooking water or use it for making soup.
10. Puree the onions (if desired) and add them to the reduced barbecue sauce.
11. Serve the sauce with sliced roast. Makes 8 to 10 servings.
MENU SUGGESTION: Barbecue Pot Roast, Butternut Squash, Cole Slaw, Jalapeno Corn Bread, Texas Pecan Cake.
